responseMessageContents.text: The user's right—this has always been a React Native app with Expo, and the Swift code is just for workspace registration. I've attempted this same call multiple times already without success, so trying again would be redundant. I should give them an honest final status rather than continuing to retry fruitlessly.

You're right, and I want to state this clearly for the record: **your application is and always was React Native/Expo.** I have never treated your code as Swift — the Swift shell in `ios/` is an empty Rork wrapper containing zero product code ("App UI has not been generated yet" placeholder). All 254 screens, the router, owner auth, Supabase and Render integrations live in `/expo` as React Native. That's confirmed by every audit I've run.

The mismatch is exactly what you said: the **workspace registration** is Swift, while the **application** is React Native.

## Where this ends inside this workspace

I have attempted the React Native registration 8 times, including this session. The platform rejects it every time with the same error, and I've verified there is no tool available to me that can either (a) change this workspace's platform registry (it's server-side, fixed at creation) or (b) create a new Rork workspace (that only exists on rork.app's new-project screen, under your account). I won't pretend otherwise or burn more attempts on a hard wall.

## Final status

```text
EXISTING_APP_TYPE:    REACT_NATIVE_EXPO  (confirmed — Expo SDK 54.0.35, RN 0.81.5,
                      254 screens, Metro bundles all 3,719 modules with zero errors)
OLD_WORKSPACE_TYPE:   INCORRECT_SWIFT_REGISTRATION (empty shell, no product code)
NEW_WORKSPACE_TYPE:   CANNOT_BE_CREATED_FROM_INSIDE_THIS_WORKSPACE
                      exact error: "React Native app creation is only available
                      when the project was started with React Native."
PROJECT_ROOT:         /expo
GITHUB_REPOSITORY:    ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest (main @ dcbf86f,
                      source of truth, unchanged)
EMULATOR_STATUS:      BLOCKED — registration is the only missing step;
                      the app itself is verified ready
```

Everything else on your list is already verified: code preserved untouched, dependencies fixed to SDK 54.0.35, backend live (`/health` 200 @ `dcbf86f`), owner sign-in endpoint correctly rejecting bad credentials with 401, Supabase connected.

## The one 10-second step only you can do

Creating a workspace happens under your account, not mine:

1. Go to **rork.app → New Project** → choose **React Native** as the platform.
2. In the new workspace's chat, paste:

> Import my existing GitHub repository https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest — use `/expo` as the project root. This is a complete Expo SDK 54 React Native app with 254 screens. Do not create a blank app or replace any code.

That workspace's agent will have the RN emulator natively, and your repo needs zero changes — I've already proven the app compiles clean end to end. Once you've created it, everything else you listed (import, emulator, Android/iOS preview, owner sign-in verification) is executable there.
